original: Conjoined twins with a fused brain successfully separated with help of virtual reality in Rio de Janeiro, Brazil
Four Colombian children are found safe after surviving 40 days in the Colombian jungle after their plane crashed killing the three adults on board
Two people are rescued after falling into a tank of chocolate at Mars M&M factory in Pennsylvania
First appearance of Donald Duck in the cartoon "The Wise Little Hen"
USSR's Vega 1 deposits lander on surface of Venus
First rocket-powered aircraft design patented byRobert Goddard
105th Belmont Stakes: Ron Turcotte aboardSecretariatwins in 2:24, becoming the first Triple Crown winner in 25 years
Les Paul [Polsfuss], American guitarist (How High The Moon), songwriter and inventor (solid-body electric guitar), born in Waukesha, Wisconsin (d. 2009)
Successful attempts to turn CO2 gases into stone underground in Iceland published in the journal "Science"
Ali Stroker becomes the first actress in a wheelchair to win a Tony award for musical "Oklahoma!"
Jurgen Hingsen of West Germany sets record for decathlon, 8,798 pts
Alice Huyler Ramsey, 22-year-old housewife from Hackensack, New Jersey, becomes the 1st woman to drive across the US, in a Maxwell 30, drives 3,800 miles from Manhattan to San Francisco in 59 days
Charles Kingsford-Smith & Charles Ulm are 1st to fly across the Pacific when they end their flight from California to Brisbane
Donald Duck's 50th birthday celebrated at Disneyland
NASA announces it will begin research into UFOs, focusing on unidentified aerial phenomena (U.A.P.s)
Patrick Steptoe, English scientist (developed in vitro fertilization), born in Oxford, England (d. 1988)
Laurie Hernandez, American gymnast (Olympic team gold, individual silver 2016), born in Old Bridge Township, New Jersey
Xolo Maridueña, American actor (Parenthood, Blue Beetle), born in Los Angeles, California
"Laserphonic Fantasy" light show premieres at Epcot
Marvel TV series "Loki" starringTom Hiddlestonpremieres on Disney+
Tomoe Hanba, Japanese voice actress (Velma Dinkley in Scooby-Doo), born in Tokyo, Japan
Johannes Gutenberg's Bible, the first book produced using the printing press, (1 of 21 complete versions) sells for $2.4 million, London
Novelty song "The Purple People Eater" by Sheb Wooley hits #1
Warner Bros "Bill of Hare" with Bug Bunny premieres in USA
Warner Bros cartoon "A Gruesome Twosome" starring Tweety premieres in the USA
British explorerMatthew Flindersarrives in Sydney becoming the first person to circumnavigate Australia, proving it is one continent
Forrest Bird, American aviator and inventor (1st respirators and ventilators), born in Stoughton, Massachusetts (d. 2015)
Elizabeth Garrett Anderson, 1st qualified woman physician in Britain, first woman mayor (Aldeburgh), born in London (d. 1917)
Johann Gottfried Galle, German astronomer (discovered Neptune), born in Radis, Kingdom of Saxony (d. 1910)
A passenger on SS Arawatta throws bottle with note overboard (found June 6, 1983 in Queensland)
One of the worst ever plagues of mice in New South Wales, Australia, prompts local government to commit $100 million in support to farmers
Patrick Kanescores the Stanley Cup-winning goal for the Chicago Blackhawks against the Philadelphia Flyers in overtime
Stanley Cup Final, Wells Fargo Center, Philadelphia, PA: Chicago Blackhawks edge Philadelphia Flyers, 4-3 in OT for a 4-2 series win; first Championship for Chicago since 1961
Stanley Cup Final, Pepsi Center, Denver, CO: Colorado Avalanche beat defending champion New Jersey Devils, 3-1 for 4-3 series win; Avalanche 2nd title
Chris Heston of the San Francisco Giants becomes first pitcher to throw a no-hitter in the 2015 MLB season; blanks New York Mets, 5-0
56th National Spelling Bee: Blake Giddens wins spelling purim
DodgerEddie MurraytiesMickey Mantlewith baseball's switch hit HRs in 10 games
Cyndi Laupergets her first US #1 hit with "Time After Time"
Mae Whitman, American Actress (The Duff), born in Los Angeles, California
George Pérez, American comic book artist (The Avenger; Teen Titans), born in New York City (d. 2022)
Anoushka Shankar, British-Indian sitar player, composer, and daughter of Ravi Shankar, born in London, England
Beth Bonner, American athlete (WR marathon 1971, 3:01:42; first winner women's division New York City Marathon 1971), born in Preston County, West Virginia (d. 1998)
Vic Zhou, Taiwanese pop singer (F4), actor (Meteor Garden; Home), and model, born in Yilan, Taiwan
Zimbabwe win their 1st one-day cricket international, v Australia
American boxerJames J. JeffriesKOs defending championBob Fitzsimmonsof England in 11th round in Brooklyn, New York to win the lineal World Heavyweight title
French explorerJacques Cartieris the 1st European to sail into mouth of St Lawrence River
US PresidentUlysses S. Grantmeets with Sioux Chief Red Cloud at The White House in Washington, D.C.
French painterPaul Gauguinarrives in Papeete, Tahiti

Thomas L. Jennings, American tailor and inventor (dry-cleaning), abolitionist, and desegregationist, born in New York City (d. 1856) [birth date is uncertain, but taken from burial record; many sources cite default of Jan. 1][1]
Bertha von Suttner, Austrian novelist and pacifist (1st woman to win the Nobel Peace Prize in 1905), born in Prague, Kingdom of Bohemia (d. 1914)
Luigi Fagioli, Italian auto racer (oldest to win F1 race at 53), born in Osimo, Ancona, Italy (d. 1952)
Gary Starkweather, American engineer and inventor of the laser printer, born in Lansing, Michigan (d. 2019)[1]
Laverne Coxbecomes the first transgender person to appear on the cover of "Time" Magazine
US Senate unanimously confirms General Charles Q. Brown (58) as Air Force Chief of Staff, 1st African American to lead a US armed forces branch
NASA suffers a launch vehicle failure launching Intelsat 509
Soyuz T-2 returns to Earth
Wreck of Dutch ship Zeewijk on the Houtman Abrolhos, Western Australia, with the loss of 72 lives, 82 survivors eventually build a boat and sail to Batavia (Jakarta)
Tony Orlando & Dawn receives gold record for "He Don't Love You"
Jon Lord, British keyboardist and composer (Deep Purple - "Smoke On The Water"; Whitesnake), born in Leicester, England (d. 2012)
Joe Haldeman, American sci-fi author (The Forever War), born in Oklahoma City
